title: Write C-LARA-2 technical report and academic papers
notes: Suggestion #8 from admin export (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-06), reprioritized on 2026-05-07 and expanded on 2026-05-09. Prepare a detailed initial C-LARA-2 technical report reflecting project status around late May or mid-June 2026, suitable as the source document for related academic publications. See docs/roadmap/reports-and-papers.md for the roadmap and target list. Current prioritized outputs are: (1) a long internal LaTeX technical report by mid-June 2026; (2) a EuroCALL 2026 paper, already accepted, with a mid-August 2026 deadline; (3) a possible ALTA 2026 paper, pending fit and deadline confirmation. Subject to co-author approval, the EuroCALL paper should focus on the user-facing half of the report, while a possible ALTA paper should focus on the implementor-facing half, with some overlap. The report should follow the C-LARA-2 AI-assisted workflow: Codex authorship with humans providing suggestions/review, and optional ChatGPT-5.x critique. A central theme should be that C-LARA-2 is not merely AI-written code: Codex has authored all committed repo content, including code, tests, roadmap documents, issue records, and operational documentation, which may be an important reason it can maintain architectural context over a rapidly growing complex platform. CodePrism currently looks like the closest known comparison point and should be investigated in detail. Priority is P1 because the internal report and accepted EuroCALL paper are active writing targets.
